questions.q: What is the Bill of Rights?
options: The first ten amendments to the Constitution
questions.explanation: The first ten amendments, ratified in 1791, protect individual liberties.

questions.q: Which freedoms does the First Amendment protect?
options: Religion, speech, press, assembly, and petition
The right to a jury trial
questions.explanation: The First Amendment protects five core freedoms.

questions.q: Protection from unreasonable search and seizure is found in which amendment?
questions.explanation: The Fourth Amendment guards against unreasonable searches and seizures.

questions.q: What does the Tenth Amendment do?
options: Reserves to the states and the people the powers not given to the federal government
Gives Congress the power to tax
questions.explanation: It reinforces federalism by reserving undelegated powers to the states and people.

questions.q: Why was the Bill of Rights added to the Constitution?
options: To reassure those who feared a too-powerful national government
questions.explanation: It placed explicit limits on government to win ratification.
